steps.title: Use the discriminant test
steps.content:
A quadratic equation has no real solutions when its discriminant is negative. For $ax^2+bx+c=0$, the discriminant is
$$b^2-4ac$$

steps.title: Check the only negative case
steps.content:
For the equation $-2x^2+2x-2=0$, use $a=-2$, $b=2$, and $c=-2$.
$$b^2-4ac=2^2-4(-2)(-2)=4-16=-12$$
Since $-12\lt 0$, this equation has no real solutions.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing the left side of each choice shows only one parabola with no $x$-intercepts. That matches the negative discriminant calculation above. The answer is D.
